channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Prosecutor says Rihanna, family were home when woman charged with attempted murder fired
item.description: Rihanna, her partner A$AP Rocky, their three children and her mother were all at home when a woman now charged with attempted murder is alleged to have fired at the property, a prosecutor said.
item.content:encoded:
Rihanna, her partner A$AP Rocky, their three children and her mother were all at home when a woman now charged with attempted murder is alleged to have fired at the property, a prosecutor said.

(Image credit: Damian Dovarganes)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Senate Democrats ramp up pressure campaign for public hearings on war with Iran
item.description: Congressional Democrats are demanding transparency in the form of public hearings from Trump administration officials on the timeline and objectives of the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Congressional Democrats are demanding transparency in the form of public hearings from Trump administration officials on the timeline and objectives of the war in Iran.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Wheelchair curler Steve Emt's path from drunk driver to three-time Paralympian
item.description: Steve Emt and Laura Dwyer represent the U.S. in the Paralympics' new mixed doubles wheelchair curling event. They could bring home Team USA's first wheelchair curling medal ever.
item.content:encoded:
Steve Emt and Laura Dwyer represent the U.S. in the Paralympics' new mixed doubles wheelchair curling event. They could bring home Team USA's first wheelchair curling medal ever.

(Image credit: Maja Hitij)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Immigration detention on track for deadliest fiscal year since 2004
item.description: Twenty-three people have died since October in ICE custody, as advocates warn about overcrowding and health care access.
item.content:encoded:
Twenty-three people have died since October in ICE custody, as advocates warn about overcrowding and health care access.

(Image credit: Rebecca Blackwell)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Photos from Iran and across the Middle East as the war enters Week 2
item.description: More than a week of the U.S. and Israel's war against Iran has dragged in global powers, upended the world's energy and transport sectors, and brought chaos to usually peaceful areas of the region.
item.content:encoded:
More than a week of the U.S. and Israel's war against Iran has dragged in global powers, upended the world's energy and transport sectors, and brought chaos to usually peaceful areas of the region.

(Image credit: Mohammad Yassine)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: A dose of psilocybin helps smokers quit in new study
item.description: The psychoactive substance in magic mushrooms appears to have a powerful effect on people trying to stop smoking.
item.content:encoded:
The psychoactive substance in magic mushrooms appears to have a powerful effect on people trying to stop smoking.

(Image credit: John Moore)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Trump gives mixed signals on Iran war. And, how Epstein built ties to scientists
item.description: President Trump provided conflicting messages about when the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran will end. And, NPR investigates how late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein leveraged ties with scientists.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump provided conflicting messages about when the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran will end. And, NPR investigates how late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein leveraged ties with scientists.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: 'Pro-worker AI,' streaming fatalities, and other fascinating new economic studies
item.description: From artificial intelligence to fatalities from music streaming to the effects of immigrants on elderly health care, the Planet Money newsletter rounds up some interesting new economic studies.
item.content:encoded:
From artificial intelligence to fatalities from music streaming to the effects of immigrants on elderly health care, the Planet Money newsletter rounds up some interesting new economic studies.

(Image credit: KIRILL KUDRYAVTSEV)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: GLP-1s have transformed weight loss and diabetes. Is addiction next?
item.description: A large study found that people taking GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic for diabetes were less likely to be diagnosed with substance use disorder.
item.content:encoded:
A large study found that people taking GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic for diabetes were less likely to be diagnosed with substance use disorder.

(Image credit: Maria Fabrizio for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: The U.S. vowed its 'most intense day of strikes inside Iran'
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the Pentagon was giving President Trump "maximum options" and that the war will not be "endless," a day after the president gave mixed signals about progress.
item.content:encoded:
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the Pentagon was giving President Trump "maximum options" and that the war will not be "endless," a day after the president gave mixed signals about progress.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
